subject: vegans
one_liner: Driverless electric pods that carry your groceries at four miles per hour and refuse, on principle, to touch anything with a face.
brand_voice_notes: Deadpan, faux-corporate, morally certain. The product is the wrong solution delivered with total confidence. It treats absurd constraints (four miles per hour, molecule purging, item ejection) as obvious engineering decisions and never acknowledges the joke. Earnest founder energy that has wandered into a hostage negotiation with its own customers.

hero.h1: Driverless delivery for people who won't eat anything with a face.
hero.sub: Husk is a fleet of unmanned electric pods that carry your groceries to your door at a respectful four miles per hour. No driver to tip. No animals harmed. No firm estimate on arrival.

features.title: The Honey Clause
features.blurb: An onboard spectrometer scans every item for trace animal products and ejects anything suspect into a sealed review drawer. It catches roughly 1.3 items per order. It is almost always the bread.

features.title: Conscience-Grade Hold
features.blurb: Each cargo bay is steam-purged between runs to remove residual non-vegan molecules. We have never detected these molecules. We remove them regardless. Certainty is not a thing you measure.

features.title: Slow on Purpose
features.blurb: Pods hold a steady four miles per hour to spare insects, pedestrians, and the climate. Delivery takes forty to ninety minutes. Anything quicker would be a statement about your values.

pricing_tiers.name: Forager
perks: Arrival shown as a range of hours

pricing_tiers.name: Cleanse
perks: Unlimited pods inside a four-mile radius

pricing_tiers.name: Pod People
perks: A pod assigned to you by name
Your pod will refuse other customers

testimonials.quote: It rejected my honey-roasted almonds and left a note. I have not stopped thinking about the note.
testimonials.name: Daria Vosk
testimonials.title: Reformed Pescatarian, Portland

testimonials.quote: The pod arrived at four miles per hour and I respect it more than most people in my life.
testimonials.name: Cormac Lindqvist
testimonials.title: Founder of a kombucha brand you have not heard of

testimonials.quote: I asked it to hurry. It did not acknowledge the request.
testimonials.name: Priya Anand-Whitlock
testimonials.title: Director of Mindful Procurement, plant-forward household of four

demo_screen_concept: A live pod-tracking dashboard for an order in transit. A map shows your pod creeping along a route at four miles per hour, taking a deliberate detour around a steakhouse. A side panel runs a live Ethics Audit that scans each item in the order; you can toggle items in or out and watch the pod accept the vegan ones and eject the questionable ones (the honey, the suspicious bread) into a review drawer with deadpan rejection notes. An ETA counter that only ever slips later. A 'nudge the pod' button that does nothing but log your impatience.

design_direction.style: Botanical seed-catalog editorial meets organic food-tech. A pale celadon paper ground with a thin double-ruled frame, hairline botanical rules, and seed-packet plate framing. A soft high-contrast serif (Fraunces) set large for the masthead with italics for taglines, a clean humanist grotesque (Hanken Grotesk) for body, and tabular monospace (Spline Sans Mono) for ETA and telemetry readouts. Engraving-style leaf and pod motifs as inline SVG. One deep botanical green carries structure; an heirloom-tomato red marks rejections and CTAs. No gradients as decoration, no glow, no blobs. Reads like a nineteenth-century seed catalog that has taken delivery of an autonomous vehicle fleet.
design_direction.archetype: Editorial product landing (seed-catalog masthead hero, feature plates, pull-quote testimonials, pricing as catalog SKUs, footer)
design_direction.motion_vocab: Restrained. Gentle fade-and-rise on scroll, a slow four-mile-per-hour marquee ribbon, hover underlines that draw in left to right, no parallax.
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Masthead-style header with a small establishment line ('Est. this morning'), an oversized serif headline left-aligned against generous celadon whitespace, an inline engraved pod-leaf plate to the right, a mono spec ribbon under the fold reading speed/range/ejection-rate, and a single tomato-red 'Summon a pod' button with a quieter 'open the demo' secondary.
